faq.cr.report.a.html: On the Workshop item page: … menu → Report. If you're the copyright holder, use Steam's formal DMCA process.
faq.cr.report.q: How do I report a violation?
faq.cr.responsibility.a.html: You are. The game provides the Workshop as a neutral platform; uploaders control and are responsible for content. Read Steam's Workshop Legal Agreement before uploading.
faq.cr.responsibility.q: Who is responsible for what I upload?
faq.cr.songs.a2.html: You cannot upload copyrighted commercial music without permission from the rights holders. Doing so leads to DMCA takedowns and Steam account penalties.
faq.cr.songs.li2: Public domain works (verify the duration in your country — typically life of author + 70 years).
faq.cr.songs.li3: Royalty-free or Creative Commons tracks (respect the license — attribute the artist in [source:]).
faq.cr.songs.q: Can I upload a song I didn't write?
faq.ed.delete.a.html: Yes. Either from in-game (your uploaded tracks), or on the Workshop page → … → Delete. Subscribers lose access immediately.
faq.ed.edit.a.html: Not directly. You can download their LRC, edit it, and re-upload as your own item with credit to the original author in the description.
faq.ed.edit.q: Can I edit someone else's upload?
faq.ed.update.a.html: Yes — open your Workshop track in-game, edit the LRC, and click Update on Workshop. Subscribers get the update automatically the next time Steam syncs.
faq.ed.update.q: Can I update my uploaded track?
faq.plat.epic.a: No — the Workshop is a Steam feature. Non-Steam players can still import local songs, but can't subscribe to or upload community tracks.
faq.plat.epic.q: Can I use the Workshop on Epic or standalone?
faq.plat.offline.a: Subscribed tracks work offline once Steam has finished downloading them. New uploads and subscriptions need an internet connection.
faq.plat.private.a.html: Yes. Pick Unlisted (accessible only by URL) or Friends only when uploading. You can flip to Public anytime from the item page on Steam.
faq.plat.private.q: Can I keep my upload private until it's ready?
faq.t.bug.a.html: Game-side: Steam discussions. Guide-side: GitHub issues.
faq.t.bug.q: I found a bug. Where do I report?
faq.t.duration.a.html: Between 2 and 6 minutes. Outside that range the import validator rejects the file — run pacing and progression are tuned around this window.
faq.t.files.a.html: Steam handles this at <Steam library>/steamapps/workshop/content/4316340/<itemid>/. You never need to touch these folders — everything routes through the game's track list.
faq.t.size.a.html: MP3s up to 50 MB. Lyrics-only uploads are typically 5–15 KB.
faq.t.unsupported.li1: Bilingual dual-line LRC (use separate uploads per language).
footer.html: Made by the Tied to the Beat community · Edit this guide on GitHub
format.basic.li3: xx — hundredths of a second, two digits (00 to 99)
format.basic.ms.html: Some editors export three-digit milliseconds (mm:ss.xxx) — that works too. Both are read correctly by the game.
format.example.intro.html: This is the exact format of the Workshop's bootstrap item Bass Boom Kids — the reference every upload follows:
format.inst.intro.html: Songs have solos, intros, outros, and breaks — parts with no vocals. Mark them explicitly with [instrumental] so the game triggers a special instrumental wave instead of falling back to default spawns:
format.inst.only.html: [instrumental] is the only marker the game reads. Other conventions you may have seen in karaoke apps ([solo], [intro], [outro], [♪], etc.) are not recognized — map all of them to [instrumental] when converting from another format.
format.langs.col1.html:
en — English
es-ES — Spanish (Spain) · Español
es-419 — Spanish (Latin America)
fr — French · Français

format.langs.col2.html:

format.langs.intro.html: Use the language code (matching the game's catalog) in the [la:...] tag:
format.langs.why.html: Why the language matters. The game maps specific words to specific gameplay effects — fire, ice, rain, lightning, love, dark, etc. Each language has its own curated dictionary. The wrong language tag means words won't trigger the right effects and your track will feel flat.
format.lead: LRC is a simple text format: one lyric line per row, each prefixed with a timestamp. Tied to the Beat uses the standard spec plus a couple of optional game-specific tags.
format.meta.intro: At the top of your file you can include ID tags. All are optional — the game auto-fills title/artist from MP3 tags — but including them in the LRC helps external tools and makes your file self-contained.
format.meta.lic.v: Short license name: Pixabay License, CC0, CC-BY 4.0, Public Domain, Own recording, …
format.meta.src.r: Yes — required on Workshop for uploads not composed by you
format.meta.src.v: Where the audio came from — direct link (Pixabay page, CC recording, your own upload, etc.)
format.mistakes.li1: Missing leading zeros — [0:12.4] breaks some parsers. Always pad to [00:12.40].
format.mistakes.li2: Wrong encoding — save as UTF-8, never ANSI. Non-Latin characters turn into ??? otherwise.
format.mistakes.li3: Blank lines with timestamps — [01:23.00] with no text is treated as end-of-line, which collapses pacing. Use [01:23.00] [instrumental] instead.
format.mistakes.li4: Duplicate timestamps — two lines with the same [mm:ss.xx]. The second one is ignored.
format.mistakes.li5: Non-monotonic timestamps — each line should start after the previous one. Going back in time confuses the game.
home.callout.copyright.html: Respect copyright. Only upload music you have rights to redistribute. Steam enforces DMCA and you are responsible for what you upload. When unsure, prefer Pixabay or your own compositions.
home.callout.steam.html: Steam only. The Workshop needs the Steam build of the game. Players on Epic or standalone can still import local songs but can't browse or subscribe to community tracks.
home.lead.html: Upload an MP3 + synchronized lyrics — everyone who owns the game can subscribe and play it. One standard format, three steps.
home.music.fma.desc: Creative Commons catalog. Attribution usually required — credit the artist in [source:].
home.music.fma.title: Free Music Archive
home.music.own.desc: Always safe. Zero legal risk, full ownership, yours to share.
home.music.own.title: Your own music
home.music.pixabay.desc.html: Easiest. Huge catalog, commercial use allowed, no attribution required.
home.music.pixabay.title: Pixabay Music
home.music.yt.desc: Curated royalty-free tracks. Usable outside YouTube. Requires a free Google account.
home.music.yt.title: YouTube Audio Library
home.step.1.desc.html: Your own music, Public Domain, or a royalty-free track from Pixabay. Anything you have rights to redistribute.
home.step.1.title: Get a shareable MP3
home.step.2.desc.html: Plain text file with song metadata and line-by-line timestamps. Use the template below — it's the exact format the game expects.
home.step.2.title: Write a lyrics.lrc
home.step.3.desc: In-game: Main menu → Import song → drop both files → review → Upload to Workshop. Steam handles the rest.
home.step.3.title: Import in-game and upload
home.template.intro.html: Copy this template, replace the values, sync your timestamps. Every Workshop item uses this exact shape — it's how the game parses metadata, triggers gameplay effects, and attributes audio sources.
home.template.outro.html: All 11 header tags explained on the format reference. The [source:] and [license:] tags are required — they're how uploaders stay honest about where their audio came from.
meta.faq.description: Answers to common questions about the Tied to the Beat community Workshop — copyright, platforms, updates, moderation.
meta.faq.title: FAQ — Tied to the Beat Workshop
meta.format.description: Complete reference for the LRC lyric file format used by Tied to the Beat — timestamps, metadata, instrumental markers, language codes.
meta.format.title: LRC format — Tied to the Beat Workshop
meta.home.description: Share and play community lyrics (LRC) and custom tracks for Tied to the Beat via Steam Workshop. One file format, three steps, no setup.
meta.home.title: Tied to the Beat — Workshop guide
